page: Google Cloud Platform n4a-highmem-16 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine configured with 16 dedicated vCPUs and 128 GB of system memory. This configuration provides a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB of RAM per core, positioning it within the memory-optimized resource class. The instance does not include local storage or bundled GPU accelerators, and it is provisioned with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Without local NVMe storage or integrated graphics processing units, this server relies entirely on network-attached storage for its operating system and data volumes. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent processing performance without resource contention from neighboring virtual machines. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the high memory density per vCPU allows workloads requiring large in-memory footprints to run on fewer compute cores, optimizing software licensing costs that are tied to core counts.

The n4a-highmem-16 is designed for memory-intensive applications that do not require local high-speed scratch disks or hardware acceleration. Suitable workloads include in-memory databases, caching layers, enterprise application servers, and medium-scale data processing pipelines where RAM capacity is the primary constraint.
description: Google Cloud Platform n4a-highmem-16 is an x86_64 virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated vCPUs and 128 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per core. This memory-optimized instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, relying on network-attached storage for data persistence. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures predictable compute performance without virtualization overhead or resource sharing. Qualitatively, this resource profile is highly cost-efficient for workloads requiring large memory footprints relative to compute requirements, allowing organizations to scale RAM without paying for unnecessary vCPUs. It is well-suited for in-memory databases, caching layers, and enterprise application servers.

tagline: A memory-optimized x86_64 virtual machine featuring sixteen dedicated vCPUs and a dense one hundred twenty-eight gigabytes of system RAM.
bullet_points: 128 GB of system memory providing 8 GB per core
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Designed for in-memory databases and caching layers
